name: Transportation & trade
description: Truck, rail, air, and freight activity plus trade volumes for transports and logistics.

series.note: Tracks volume of freight hauled by motor carriers, the broadest read on domestic goods demand and trucking utilization.
Cass-style shipments proxy aggregating North American freight activity across modes, a leading demand signal for the whole freight cycle.
BTS index of for-hire freight movement across all modes, a clean monthly gauge of physical goods flow and logistics throughput.
Measures bulk/commodity rail volumes (coal, chemicals, grain, autos), a direct read on heavy-industry and commodity demand for the rails.
Tracks containerized intermodal volumes tied to imports and retail inventory flows, the rail growth engine most sensitive to consumer goods.
Core airline demand metric capturing paid passenger travel volume, the top-line driver for carrier revenue and capacity decisions.
Aggregate U.S. road travel, a demand proxy for fuel consumption, toll/road usage, and consumer mobility.
Headline net trade balance driving the external sector of GDP and signaling import/export momentum for trade-exposed sectors.
Inflation-adjusted exports, a demand gauge for U.S.-made goods and a barometer for global trade and industrial exporters.
Inflation-adjusted imports, a leading signal for port volumes, intermodal rail, and consumer-goods supply chains.
Bilateral China import flows that drive trans-Pacific container traffic and reveal supply-chain reshoring/tariff effects.
Transportation PPI capturing truckload pricing/rate trends, a direct margin and shipper-cost signal for the trucking cycle.
Tracks rail pricing power, a core driver of railroad revenue per carload and pricing-led margin expansion.
Proxy for airfare/yield trends, informing airline revenue-per-mile and pricing power across the cycle.
Diesel is the dominant variable cost for trucking and rail, making this a key margin and fuel-surcharge input.
Trucking payroll trends signal capacity expansion/contraction and labor-cost pressure in the largest freight mode.
Broad sector employment gauge for capacity and labor costs across freight, parcel, and warehousing.
The inventory-to-sales ratio leads restocking cycles that drive freight demand swings across trucking and intermodal.
